data.title: An address transferred 5.05 million ENA to Binance 2 minutes ago, equivalent to about 2.349 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News On-chain data shows that a certain address transferred 5.05 million ENA to Binance 10 minutes ago, worth about $2,349,568.05. Currently, the address still holds about 5.1 million ENA, worth about $2.4 million.

data.title: The address that hoarded 11.5 million OM tokens 11 months ago transferred all the tokens to Binance, and the sale will make a profit of US$77 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Spot On Chain monitoring, the smart money address "0x177e6" earned $77 million by trading OM in less than a year. The address hoarded 11.5 million OM through Binance in March 2024, with an average price of $0.63 and a cost of $6.9 million.
Today, OM rose to $7.53. The address began to transfer these tokens to Binance through multiple addresses in the past 24 hours. If all are sold, it will make a profit of $84.5 million, with a profit of $77 million.

data.title: Data: Bitcoin's average transaction fee drops to lowest level in recent years
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News CryptoQuant analyst Maartunn wrote that the average transaction fee for Bitcoin is currently 0.000013 BTC (about $1.27), reaching its lowest point in many years. Bitcoin transaction fees have soared several times in May 2023, December 2023, and April 2024. These increases were mainly driven by the launch of the Runes protocol and the sharp rise in Bitcoin prices. At that time, the number of unconfirmed transactions in the mempool (transaction memory pool) increased significantly, causing users to pay higher fees for faster confirmation. Today, the mempool is idle, the popularity of Bitcoin Inscriptions has declined, and many retail investors seem to have exited the market again. Now is a great time to optimize wallet management. If your Bitcoin is scattered across multiple addresses, now is a low-cost opportunity to merge them.

data.title: Bitcoin's Inter-Exchange Flow Pulse Turns Bearish
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on February 15, CryptoQuant analyst Maartunn reported that the Bitcoin Inter-Exchange Flow Pulse (IFP) indicator has turned bearish. This indicator utilizes CryptoQuant's data on Bitcoin exchange flows to assess the movement of funds between spot and derivatives trading platforms. The flow data reflects market sentiment:

- An increase in Bitcoin inflow to derivatives platforms is typically associated with bullish market conditions.

- A decrease in Bitcoin outflow from derivatives platforms is usually linked to bearish market conditions.

data.title: Bitcoin Transaction Fees Reach Multi-Year Low
data.description: According to PANews, CryptoQuant analyst Maartunn reports that Bitcoin's average transaction fee has dropped to 0.000013 BTC, approximately $1.27, marking a multi-year low. In May 2023, December 2023, and April 2024, Bitcoin transaction fees surged significantly, driven by the launch of the Runes protocol and substantial increases in Bitcoin's price. During these periods, the number of unconfirmed transactions in the mempool rose sharply, prompting users to pay higher fees for faster confirmations. Currently, the mempool is idle, and the popularity of Bitcoin inscriptions has waned. This situation presents a low-cost opportunity for consolidating funds if Bitcoin is distributed across multiple addresses.
